message:
fix(codegen): Fix lambda-in-call expression statement producing orphan semicolon

Root cause: The unittest method dispatch for self.addCleanup() was
being applied to ALL classes with methods named addCleanup, not just
unittest.TestCase subclasses. The genAddCleanup no-op produced nothing,
leaving only the semicolon from the expression statement wrapper.

Two fixes applied:
1. Only dispatch to UnittestMethods when inside a TestCase subclass
   (checked via isTestCaseSubclass in method_calls.zig)
2. Make genAddCleanup emit {} instead of nothing, which the expression
   statement handler recognizes and skips entirely (including semicolon)

This fixes patterns like:
  self.addCleanup(lambda: rmtree(tempdir))

Which previously generated just ";" and now correctly generates either
the actual method call (non-unittest classes) or nothing (unittest).
